turns.text: no okay alright and well the doctor the doctor wan na go ahead let the doctor just do a quick physical exam the doctor is gon na be calling out some of the doctor's exam findings and the doctor'll let michelle know what the doctor's exam findings means when the doctor is done okay alright so on michelle's heart exam michelle's heart sounds in a nice regular rate and rhythm the doctor do n't appreciate any murmur on michelle's lung exam michelle's lungs are nice and clear to auscultation and remind the doctor what knee did michelle injure again

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: okay there is pain to palpation of michelle's right lateral knee there is decreased flexion and extension there is a positive lachman sign there is a palpable palpable dorsalis pedis and posterior tibial pulse there is no leg edema in the ankle okay well let's just talk a little bit about michelle you know the doctor's assessment and you know the doctor's plan for michelle so you know the doctor knows that michelle had the x-ray done of michelle's right knee the x-ray of michelle's right knee did n't show any bony abnormality but the doctor the doctor 'm concerned that michelle have ruptured michelle's acl or michelle's anterior cruciate ligament michelle's anterior cruciate ligament is like a major ligament that helps connect and helps michelle's knee move back and forth so the doctor wan na go ahead and order a knee mri just so that the doctor and michelle can get a a a good look and just you know confirm that physical exam okay now some people can have a normal physical exam and the people's acl can still be torn but michelle do have a lot of pain on the lateral aspect of michelle's knee so the doctor wan na make sure if there make sure that there is not any other structures that have been damaged by this accident okay the doctor wan na go ahead and you know is michelle what is michelle taking for the pain

turns.speaker: P

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: alright so let's just continue with ibuprofen michelle can take you know six hundred to eight hundred milligrams every eight hours as needed the doctor wan na go ahead and put michelle in a brace the brace is gon na help michelle's knee feel a bit more supported okay and let's go ahead and the doctor 'm hoping that the doctor and michelle can get the knee mri done in you know the next couple days and then the doctor and michelle can have a conversation about what needs to be done now michelle said that michelle are are are you know obviously a very active active mom any other exercise or anything else that the doctor should be aware of that michelle do just wondering in terms of which kind of graft the doctor would use to fix the acl injury with michelle's acl being injured
